name: rad-session
description: Three-phase Claude Code workflow lifecycle with cross-machine continuity and slim wrapup: /init bootstraps a project once (deterministic stack detection via Python scripts, CLAUDE.md scaffold, gitignore handling for session files, rad-* plugin recommendations, plus 3.6 plugin-bloat audit that proposes per-project enabledPlugins disables), /startup orients each session (Phase 0 fetches origin and prompts to pull when behind BEFORE reading any handoff file — listing incoming commits first; --auto-pull / --no-pull flag overrides; cross-machine handoff detection via hostname; parallel-batched resource discovery; stale-handoff auto-refresh from git log), /wrapup captures each session (recency-bounded conversation tagging, structured HANDOFF.md with 'what NOT to do' field, mechanical session-log derivation from HANDOFF, CLAUDE.md prune auto-skipped when unchanged, Phase 6 auto-commit + prompted push of session files with --push / --no-push / --quick overrides), /add-resource registers tools any time. Three Python scripts (detect-stack.py, detect-resources.py, audit-plugin-bloat.py) make stack, MCP, and plugin-bloat detection deterministic. PreCompact hook prevents silent context loss. 3.6 adds /init Step 5.5: per-project plugin-bloat audit. New audit-plugin-bloat.py script detects 10 stack signals (supabase, stripe, coolify, chrome_extension, frontend_web, python, anthropic_sdk, 1password_secrets, claude_plugin_repo, content_site) and applies a built-in catalog of plugin-relevance rules to recommend which installed plugins to disable per-project via the enabledPlugins map in .claude/settings.local.json. Categories: core (always keep), stack-conditional (signal-gated), productivity (default disable), meta-authoring (plugin-authoring repos only). User-scope plugin enables stay intact in other projects. Saves both MCP-registry tokens (per turn) and skill-listing tokens (per turn) for projects that don't use a given plugin's stack. 3.5.2 makes the /wrapup final state block anomaly-gated — success path emits one line ('Session wrapped up. Sync: pushed (sha).'); the verbose block surfaces only when something needs action (over-cap files, trap promotion, CLAUDE.md actually pruned, push failed). Silent-skip protection from 3.4 is preserved — Bash size checks still run every wrapup, anomalies still always surface — but the wrapup stops reading itself back when there's nothing to act on. 3.5.1 removed the dead-code 'surface insights' phase from /wrapup (empirical evidence: 12+ wrapups in one project produced zero saved memory files). 3.5.0 cut /startup and /wrapup wall-clock time by 4–5× via per-skill model pinning to Haiku 4.5, mtime-keyed cache for detect-resources.py output (.claude/cache/resources.json — invalidated automatically on any input edit), and a low-activity auto-quick short-circuit in /wrapup Phase 1.3 (sessions with <10 substantive turns since last signal automatically apply --quick semantics). 3.4 closed the silent-skip-of-maintenance defect: Phase 3 split into 3.A (append) and 3.B (mandatory programmatic Bash-gated maintenance — never conditional on LLM judgment), per-bullet length caps on HANDOFF (≤3 sentences) and session-log (≤1 sentence per bullet, hard cap 30 lines / 1.5 KB per entry).
